title: Ecotone follow-on prep after Treeline
summary: Tenth structured packet for Eco Explorer. Treeline is now scaffolded in isolation, so the next useful parallel work is prep for the five-biome journal selector and the shared-species layer, plus one small science-model cleanup so lichens are not mislabeled once Treeline goes live.

execution_order.goal: Prepare a compact handoff for the five-biome journal selector before main-21 starts reshaping the journal top bar.
execution_order.why_now: The selector is the next visible scaling risk once both ecotones exist, and a scout comparison can save iteration time.

execution_order.goal: Prepare a shared-species handoff for main-17 so IDs, ownership, and migration boundaries are clear before cross-biome deduplication work starts.
execution_order.why_now: The project now has real shared edge species on both sides of the world chain, so a clean source-of-truth plan will reduce drift.

execution_order.goal: Add lichen-safe category handling before Treeline is wired into the live travel flow.
execution_order.why_now: Treeline introduced a real lichen entry, and the current journal/field-guide category model still calls it a plant.

guardrails: Keep the next journal selector readable at 192x144 and kid-comprehensible in one glance.
Shared-species prep should define ownership and IDs clearly without prematurely rewriting live content modules.
The lichen fix should stay lightweight and should not explode the category model or the UI.
Science accuracy remains a hard gate for the ecotone chain.
acceptance_focus: Scout handoffs should make main-21 and main-17 faster to start, not add abstract churn.
Treeline should stop teaching that lichens are plants before it becomes part of the live five-biome route.
The queue should remain role-routable and easy for new agents to follow.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-treeline-review.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-readability-pass-review.md, docs/ecotone-design.md, docs/science-source-ledger.md, and this packet. Use it to prep the next journal-selector and shared-species handoffs, and to preserve the lichen-safe follow-up before Treeline is wired live.
